#!/bin/bash
set -e
# This wrapper compiles to .bc and also to .o via llc
# Expects arguments like -c file.c -o file.o
# We'll parse to get output file.
# We'll run clang with -emit-llvm -c to produce .bc, then llc to produce .o
# Keep all other flags.
# We'll also need to handle -o flag.
args=()
input_file=""
output_file=""
in_c=0
for arg in "$@"; do
    if [ "$arg" = "-c" ]; then
        in_c=1
        args+=("$arg")
    elif [ "$arg" = "-o" ]; then
        in_o=1
        args+=("$arg")
    elif [ $in_o -eq 1 ]; then
        output_file="$arg"
        args+=("$arg")
        in_o=0
    elif [ $in_c -eq 1 ] && [ -z "$input_file" ]; then
        input_file="$arg"
        args+=("$arg")
        in_c=0
    else
        args+=("$arg")
    fi
done
# Run clang with -emit-llvm -c to produce .bc
if [ -n "$input_file" ] && [ -n "$output_file" ]; then
    # Determine base name
    bc_file="${output_file%.o}.bc"
    # compile to bc
    clang -emit-llvm "${@/-c/-c}" -o "$bc_file" 2>&1
    # compile to object (using llc)
    llc -filetype=obj "$bc_file" -o "$output_file" 2>&1
else
    # Not a compile command, just pass through
    exec clang "$@"
fi
EOF
",

    }
  ],
  "task_complete": false
}

#!/bin/bash
# Wrapper for clang that generates .bc files alongside .o files.
# We'll parse arguments to detect compile mode.

args=("$@")

input=""
output=""
lo=0
for (( i=0; i<${#args[@]}; i++ )); do

    if [[ "$arg" == "-c" ]]; then
        compile=1
    elif [[ "$arg" == "-o" ]]; then
        next=$((i+1))
